How's it going everyone? In this video I'm going to go through Google Sheets, which is a spreadsheet program as part of Google. And that's something similar to Excel with Microsoft Office or Numbers on a Mac. It's a program that lets you store information. And they can get really in depth and complicated, but we're going to try to only use some basic features. That way it's not overwhelming and because we don't really need them right now. If you don't already have a Google account, you do need to sign up for one. So just go to Google.com. At the top right corner you'll have a sign up. Go ahead and click on that sign up and create one. It'll give you a new email address. It'll say at gmail.com, whatever you pick. And that's your Google account. So just always make sure you know that email address and that password and that way you can always use it if you need to in the future. Now once you're inside of Google Drive, that's just drive.google.com. In the address bar, to enter, it'll take you right here. You'll see this kind of view. Now I have a bunch of files here. It's not very organized. This is just my junk account pretty much. You can create different folders. You can create different files. You can do quite a lot with this. It's like a file manager like Finder or Windows Explorer, where all of your files are contained inside of folders. You can move things around. What I'm going to show you is how to create a basic spreadsheet. At the top left, we want to click on this new button. And once we're inside new, you see a few different options. And upload files or folders, create a new folder. Docs is like a Microsoft Word document. Slides is like PowerPoint. But we're going to go and click on Google Sheets right there. It's going to immediately create a new blank Google Sheet for us, but it doesn't have a name. So the first thing that we want to do is put a name in there. So click on untitled spreadsheet up at the top left and give it a name. And it can be whatever makes sense to you. That way you know what is later on. I'm just going to call this test spreadsheet. Now the basic layout, you have your menu here. You have different tools here. You have your columns. And these are alphabetical, ABCD, all the way through. If you get to the end, you get to Z, it gives you AA, AB, AC. Hopefully you'll never have to have that many column headers. The row number is over here on the left. And if you click on a column right there, I'll select the entire column. You click on a row, it'll click the entire row. Now you want to be careful once you set up a spreadsheet to not accidentally click and drag something out of place, because that could break how data is going in there. So just be very careful. And always remember, you can do edit, undo, and they also give you an undo arrow right there. The first row is going to be called your header, or heading. That's going to be just the title of whatever information is in that column. So I'm going to go and call this one first name. Last name, email, those are really the only four we use for the most part, you may end up needing other ones. And we'll help you show you, help show you when those happen. Like if you want to do way in appointment, and that's something will help you set up automatically through acuity, or if you want to manually track it, that way you have one place to call it all. Now one thing to keep in mind is you can have multiple worksheets inside, that's kind of like different tabs within it. At the bottom here, you see where it says sheet one, that's the worksheet name, I'm going to go and give that a better name by clicking on that twice, I'm going to type in leave. That way I know what the sheet is. If I need to add in more worksheets, I can click that plus sign right there, it'll give me more. If I create one too many, I can click the little arrow next to it, and I can delete it. Just make sure you aren't deleting a sheet that you want to keep, it gives you this confirmation to help me protect yourself. So now that we have some column headers in there, I want to make it to where even if I scroll down, those column headers stay there. So it makes it easier to find out what informations what. So I'm going to click this one, and that's going to select the entire row. I'm going to click on view and freeze. I'm going to do one row. And so now that's frozen. So if I scroll down, it's going to show up there the entire time. If I click it again, it selects everything I can make a bolt so that way it stands out. You can do a couple different things with this. That's just a basic rundown of Google Sheets. It's a very complex program if you get into it. Hopefully you won't have to too much, but you can do a lot of cool things with it if you spend the time to learn about it. That's it for this video. I hope you enjoyed it and hope you enjoy the Mac.
